news.title: GAD Kicks Off the 18-Day Campaign to End VAW
news.excerpt: The GAD Focal Point System leads the Orange Wave campaign with advocacy activities across the campus.
news.body: In observance of the National and International 18-Day Campaign to End Violence Against Women (VAW), the CvSU-CCAT GAD office spearheaded a series of advocacy activities. The Orange Wave campaign underscores the university's continuing commitment to protect the dignity, safety, and equality of every student and employee.

news.title: IEC Distribution Strengthens Safe Spaces Awareness
news.excerpt: Students and personnel received Safe Spaces and anti-sexual harassment IEC materials.
news.body: On February 6 and 10, 2025, the GAD office conducted an Information, Education, and Communication (IEC) materials distribution. The activity raised awareness on RA 11313 (Safe Spaces Act), RA 7877 (Anti-Sexual Harassment Act), and the CvSU CODI mechanism for reporting gender-based sexual harassment.

news.title: GAD Webinar Promotes Gender-Responsive Learning
news.excerpt: Highlights from the GAD webinar on building inclusive and gender-fair spaces.
news.body: The GAD Focal Point System organized a webinar focused on gender sensitivity, gender-fair language, and building inclusive learning and working environments. Participants gained practical tools for mainstreaming gender and development in their respective units.
news.image: /2025 PHOTOS/Copy of Copy of HIGHLIGHTS FO THE WEBINAR.png

news.title: Women's Empowerment and Leadership Activities
news.excerpt: Celebrating the contributions of women in the CvSU-CCAT community.
news.body: As part of the National Women's Month Celebration, the GAD office recognized the invaluable roles and contributions of women within the university, the family, community, and society, in line with the Magna Carta of Women.

agenda.title: GAD Agenda
agenda.body: The CvSU-CCAT GAD Agenda sets the strategic direction for gender mainstreaming, guided by the Magna Carta of Women (RA 9710), the Safe Spaces Act (RA 11313), and the Anti-Sexual Harassment Act (RA 7877). It focuses on protecting rights, promoting equal opportunity, and institutionalizing gender-responsive policies, programs, and services.
points: Institutionalize gender mainstreaming in instruction, research, extension, and administration.
Protect students and personnel through Safe Spaces and anti-GBSH mechanisms.
Promote women's empowerment, leadership, and participation in decision-making.

estadoNiJuana.title: Estado ni Juana Report
estadoNiJuana.body: The Estado ni Juana Report details the campus's achievements in gender mainstreaming and the impact of GAD Programs, Activities, and Projects (PAPs) on women and girls, with particular attention to the benefits for women and girls from underserved communities and marginalized sectors.

about.vision: A gender-responsive university where every member of the community learns, works, and grows in a safe, inclusive, and equal environment.
about.mission: To mainstream gender and development in instruction, research, extension, and administration, and to protect the rights, dignity, and welfare of all genders.
about.committee: The GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) manages and updates the physical and digital GAD Corner, ensuring content remains current, relevant, and compliant with PCW guidelines.
